title: Run schema changes with ALGORITHM=INPLACE online DDL
description: Many InnoDB ALTERs can run without a full table copy and without blocking writes when the right algorithm is chosen.
body: Use when altering a populated InnoDB table that takes live traffic. Do not let an ALTER silently fall back to ALGORITHM=COPY, which rebuilds the table under a metadata lock and blocks writes for the whole copy. Actions: declare ALGORITHM=INPLACE, LOCK=NONE explicitly so the statement fails fast if the operation cannot be done online, prefer instant column adds on MySQL 8, and inspect performance_schema for the stage. Validation signals include writes continuing during the change and the ALTER reporting an inplace, non-copy path. Risk boundary: a forced COPY rebuild on a hot table is an availability event, so schedule a window and use a tool like gh-ost or pt-online-schema-change with a human gate.
